header.title: The white noise app that works like a sound machine — and fades out while you dream
header.tagline: Sound Machine Deep Sleep DRMN plays white noise, rain, fan, and deep sleep sounds on your iPhone. Mix your own soundscape, set a fade-out timer up to 10 hours, and keep listening offline — no bedside machine required.
header.tldr: DRMN is a free white noise and sleep sound machine app for iPhone (iOS 17.1+). It plays white, brown, and pink noise, rain, fan, and nature sounds, mixes multiple tracks, works offline, and fades out with a sleep timer of up to 10 hours. App Store reviewers report falling asleep faster when background noise is the problem; results vary by person.

features.title: Sleep Sounds & Nature Sounds for Better Rest

items.title: Rich Library of Sleep Sounds
items.description: Choose from white noise, brown noise, and dark noise for deep relaxation. Explore nature sounds like gentle rain, ocean waves, crackling fire, and morning birds. We also have ambient meditation tracks and soft instrumentals. Mix and match sounds to create your perfect sleep environment.

items.title: Fall Asleep Faster, Sleep Deeper
items.description: Steady background sound masks traffic, neighbors, and snoring, so sudden noises are less likely to wake you. A consistent audio environment also gives your brain a familiar cue that it's time to rest. Pick one track or build a mix that matches how you fall asleep.

items.title: Focus Better, Work Smarter
items.description: Studying, deep work, or a noisy coffee shop: steady sound covers conversations and clatter so you can concentrate. Brown noise and ambient tracks work well for long focus sessions, and you can keep a separate mix saved just for work.

items.title: Help Your Baby Sleep Peacefully
items.description: Baby sleep sounds like hair dryer, vacuum cleaner, and keyboard typing give infants and toddlers a familiar, steady rhythm to settle to. Play them from your iPhone in the nursery instead of buying a separate bedside sound machine.

items.title: White Noise Sounds
items.description: Steady white noise masks sudden disturbances—traffic, neighbors, or a creaky house—and gives your brain a predictable backdrop for rest. Preview fan-style hums and classic static, then mix them with rain or ocean waves for a custom bedtime blend.

items.title: Brown Noise Sounds
items.description: Brown and dark noise emphasize lower frequencies. Many people find them warmer and less sharp than white noise, especially for deep sleep or focus. Try brown noise alone, or layer it under nature sounds when you need extra masking.

items.title: Baby Sleep Sounds
items.description: Gentle hair-dryer, vacuum, and keyboard-style tracks mimic familiar rhythms that soothe infants and toddlers. Parents use them to calm nursery noise and help little ones settle—without buying a separate bedside sound machine.

items.title: Sleep Timer Features
items.description: Set a timer with automatic fade-out so audio tapers off after you fall asleep. The free version includes a one-hour timer—enough for most nights—while premium unlocks longer sessions when you want all-night playback.

items.title: Offline Sound Machine
items.description: Download your favorite tracks once, then play up to 10 hours without Wi‑Fi or cellular data. Battery optimization keeps playback running through the night—ideal for travel, camping, or bedrooms with weak signal.

app_store_proof.title: Rated on the App Store
app_store_proof.description: Ratings and reviews come straight from the App Store. Read what users say before you download.

about.title: About DRMN
about.description: DRMN is an independent iOS sleep and focus app built by Vladimir Ivakhnenko. It started as a simple tool for better rest—steady sounds, offline playback, and no subscription required to try the core features.

content.title: A Sound Machine App for Sleep, Focus, and Baby Rest
content.intro: DRMN is a white noise and sleep sound machine app for iPhone with rain, fan, brown noise, and nature sounds. It has over 10,000 downloads and a 4.9-star App Store rating, and covers three main uses: masking noise so you fall asleep faster, backgrounding deep work, and settling babies in the nursery.

sections.title: A Sound Library Designed for Your Needs
sections.paragraph1: We've carefully curated a collection of high-quality sounds to help you relax, focus, and sleep better:
list_items: White noise, brown noise, and dark noise - Perfect for masking disruptive sounds and creating a consistent sleep environment
Nature sounds - Rain sound for sleep, ocean waves, crackling fire, fan sound for sleep, and morning birds transport you to a peaceful place
Meditation and focus sounds - Ambient tracks and soft instrumentals help you concentrate during work or practice mindfulness
Baby sleep sounds - Familiar sounds like hair dryer, vacuum cleaner, and keyboard typing help little ones drift off to sleep
sections.paragraph2: You can mix any of these together: white noise under ocean waves, rain over soft meditation music, or whatever combination helps you switch off. Mixing is unlimited and included in the free version.

sections.title: How Sound Masking Helps You Fall Asleep
sections.paragraph1: Sleep research on noise masking points to a simple mechanism: your brain reacts to changes in sound more than to sound itself. Steady audio helps in three ways:
list_items: Masks disruptive noise - Traffic, neighbors, snoring, and sudden sounds blend into the background instead of standing out
Signals your brain to rest - The same track every night becomes a cue that it's time to wind down
Reduces nighttime awakenings - A constant sound floor makes you less likely to be startled awake by one-off noises
sections.paragraph2: App Store reviewers most often mention falling asleep faster when background noise was the thing keeping them up. Results vary by person, so preview a few tracks and give one a full week before judging it.

sections.title: Focus Sounds for Work and Study
sections.paragraph1: The same masking that helps at night works during the day. If you study in a shared space, work from a coffee shop, or sit in an open office, steady sound covers the conversations and clatter that break concentration:
list_items: Brown noise for deep work - Lower frequencies feel warmer than white noise and are easier to listen to for hours
Ambient and instrumental tracks - Background texture without lyrics that pull your attention
Saved mixes - Keep one blend for sleep and a separate one for work sessions
Offline playback - Focus sounds keep playing on a plane or anywhere without signal
sections.paragraph2: Reviewers who use DRMN during the day mention studying, open-plan offices, and writing sessions as the most common use cases.

sections.title: Baby Sleep Sounds for the Nursery
sections.paragraph1: DRMN includes sounds parents already know work: hair dryer, vacuum hum, and keyboard typing. These steady, low-variation rhythms resemble the constant background noise babies heard in the womb, which is why they settle infants when silence doesn't.
sections.paragraph2: Set the timer if you only need sound while your baby falls asleep, or use offline playback for all-night sessions without keeping the phone online. An old iPhone in the nursery running DRMN replaces a dedicated bedside sound machine.

sections.title: Built with Care, Designed for Real Life
sections.paragraph1: We've thought through every detail to make DRMN work seamlessly in your daily routine:
list_items: Professionally recorded sounds - Over 100 hours of carefully recorded and mastered audio for the best quality
Mix and match - Combine different sounds to create your perfect blend
Smart sleep timer - Set it to fade out automatically so you don't have to worry about it playing all night
Simple, beautiful interface - Start sleeping better in seconds, no complicated setup needed
Works offline - Download your favorite sounds and enjoy up to 10 hours of uninterrupted playback anywhere, even without internet
Battery-friendly - Optimized to run all night without draining your iPhone battery
Free to start - Download now and try it free, with optional premium features available
sections.paragraph2: DRMN works on iPhone with iOS 17.1 or later and is optimized for all iPhone models. The free version includes a 1-hour timer (perfect for falling asleep), offline mode, sound mixing, and battery optimization. Premium features unlock extended playback and additional sounds.

faq.question: What is the best white noise app for iPhone?
faq.answer: A good white noise app needs four things: gapless looping, background playback with the screen locked, a fade-out timer, and offline support. DRMN does all four and adds a sound mixer, so you can layer white noise with rain or fan sounds.

faq.question: Can a phone app replace a sleep sound machine?
faq.answer: Yes. A sound machine app plays the same steady masking audio as a bedside device, adds mixing and fade timers most hardware lacks, travels with you, and skips the $50–$100 price tag.

faq.question: What are the best sounds for sleep?
faq.answer: Steady, unchanging sounds work best: white, pink, or brown noise, rain, fan hum, and ocean waves. They mask the sudden noises — doors, traffic, snoring — that actually wake you. The right one is personal, so test each for a full night.

faq.question: How do sleep sounds help you fall asleep faster?
faq.answer: Steady sound raises the threshold a noise needs to cross to alert your brain, and a consistent bedtime sound becomes a conditioning cue — after about a week, your brain links it to falling asleep.

faq.question: Why do rain sounds help you sleep?
faq.answer: Rain is natural broadband noise close to pink noise: it masks disruptions without sounding artificial, and most people find it calming. Layer it with brown noise to cover low-frequency sounds too.

faq.question: Can sleep sounds turn off automatically with a timer?
faq.answer: Yes — DRMN’s sleep timer runs from a few minutes up to 10 hours and ends with a gradual fade-out, so the stop never startles you awake the way a hard cutoff can.

faq.question: Is white noise safe for babies?
faq.answer: Yes, when kept at or below roughly 50 decibels with the phone at least 7 feet (2 meters) from the crib, per pediatric guidance. Use a timer rather than loud all-night playback, and never put the device in the crib.

faq.question: How do I block out snoring at night?
faq.answer: Snoring is low-frequency, so high-pitched white noise misses it. Use brown noise or fan sound — or rain layered over brown noise — set just above the snoring volume, with a long timer.

faq.question: Can I get a fan sound for sleep without running a fan?
faq.answer: Yes. DRMN plays a gapless fan hum with no cold air, no dust, and no fan running all night. Mix it with white noise and set a fade timer.

faq.question: What sounds are best for deep sleep?
faq.answer: Lower-frequency sounds — brown noise, pink noise, ocean waves — mask disruptions without harsh high frequencies, which makes them the usual picks for deep sleep support. Keep the volume at conversation level or below.

faq.question: Is there a free white noise app for iPhone?
faq.answer: Yes. DRMN is free to download on the App Store. The free tier includes a sleep timer, offline playback, sound mixing, and core sleep sounds. Premium unlocks longer sessions and additional tracks — no subscription required to try the basics.

faq.question: Does DRMN work offline?
faq.answer: Yes. After you download tracks, DRMN plays without internet. Battery optimization supports up to 10 hours of uninterrupted playback — enough for travel or all-night use.

faq.question: Who makes DRMN?
faq.answer: DRMN is developed by Vladimir Ivakhnenko, an independent iOS developer. The app is listed on the App Store with a 4.9-star rating and more than 10,000 downloads. Contact: c-basso@ya.ru.

howto.name: How to Use DRMN for Better Sleep
howto.description: Fall asleep faster with ambient sound on iPhone: pick a sound, mix your soundscape, set a fade-out timer, and press play.

step.name: Download DRMN
step.text: Get DRMN free from the App Store on iPhone (iOS 17.1 or later). The free tier includes a one-hour timer and core sounds.

step.name: Browse the sound library
step.text: Open DRMN and explore white noise, brown noise, rain, ocean waves, fire, meditation tracks, and baby sleep sounds.

step.name: Choose sounds for your needs
step.text: Pick steady noise to mask disruptions, nature sounds to relax, or baby sounds for nursery use. Preview any track before playing.

step.name: Mix sounds (optional)
step.text: Layer white noise with ocean waves, or rain with soft meditation audio, to build a custom bedtime blend.

step.name: Set a sleep timer
step.text: Turn on the timer with auto-fade if you do not want audio all night. Audio tapers off after you drift off.

step.name: Play and adjust volume
step.text: Start playback and set volume high enough to mask noise but low enough to stay comfortable. Phone on the nightstand or headphones both work.

step.name: Download for offline use
step.text: Save favorite tracks for offline playback—useful when traveling or when your bedroom has weak Wi‑Fi.

how_it_works.title: From “can’t sleep” to asleep in four taps
how_it_works.intro: Steady sound masks the sudden noises that wake you — doors, traffic, snoring. Here is the whole routine.

steps.title: Pick a sound
steps.description: Choose white, brown, or pink noise, rain, ocean, fan, or nature ambience from the library.

steps.title: Mix your soundscape
steps.description: Layer tracks in the mixer — rain over brown noise, thunder under ocean waves — with per-track volume.

steps.title: Set the timer
steps.description: Pick anything up to 10 hours. Playback fades out gradually so the stop never startles you awake.

steps.title: Press play and sleep
steps.description: Lock the screen and put the phone down. Sounds keep playing all night — even offline.
